0,"Below is a **budget‑friendly, 7‑day meal plan** that meets all of your constraints and flavor preferences.
It is **gluten‑free, lactose‑free, shellfish‑free, and onion‑free**, while still delivering the bold, “super‑tasting” profiles you love (spicy Mexican, aromatic Thai, fragrant Indian).
## QUICK‑LOOK NUTRITION (≈ 2 000 kcal / day)

## KEY INGREDIENTS (buy in bulk to keep cost low)

column3: Chicken thighs (bone‑in, skin‑on), ground turkey or beef (≈ 80 % lean), pork shoulder, canned lentils, canned chickpeas, eggs, tofu (firm, soy‑based)
White rice, brown rice, potatoes, sweet potatoes, corn tortillas (gluten‑free), quinoa, gluten‑free oats
Frozen mixed Asian veg (broccoli, carrots, snap peas), fresh carrots, bell peppers, zucchini, cabbage, spinach, canned diced tomatoes, frozen peas

Olive oil, coconut oil, peanut oil, avocado oil, canned coconut milk, nuts (peanuts, almonds), seeds (pumpkin, sunflower), fresh herbs (cilantro, basil, mint), lime, lemon, garlic, ginger, fresh chilies, dried spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, smoked paprika, chili powder, curry powder, Thai red‑curry paste – check label for gluten), tamari (gluten‑free soy sauce), fish sauce (use sparingly), rice‑vinegar, honey or maple syrup (just a drizzle for balance)
Plain popcorn kernels, rice cakes (gluten‑free), fruit (bananas, apples), Greek‑style lactose‑free yogurt, handful of nuts, boiled eggs

column1: ## DAILY MENU
> **Format:**  Breakfast → Lunch → Dinner → 2 Snacks (mid‑morning & afternoon)
> **Prep tips:**
> • Cook a large batch of rice (or quinoa) on **Sunday** – store in the fridge for the week.
> • Roast a tray of potatoes & sweet potatoes (season lightly) – they keep 4‑5 days.
> • Boil a pot of lentils/chickpeas (or use canned) – rinse well.
> • Keep a “spice jar” of your favorite blends (Mexican, Thai, Indian) ready to toss in.
### **Day 1 – Mexican‑Style**

column4:
• ½ cup cooked rice
• 2 eggs scrambled with a pinch of smoked paprika, cumin, and a dash of tamari
• ¼ cup black beans (rinsed)
• Diced tomato, sliced avocado, fresh cilantro, lime wedge.
1 medium apple, 1 Tbsp natural peanut butter.

• 150 g cooked shredded chicken thigh (seasoned with chili powder, cumin, garlic powder)
• Large butter‑lettuce leaves (or Romaine)
• Corn tortilla strips (quick‑fried in a splash of oil, lightly salted)
• Pico de gallo (tomato, jalapeño, cilantro, lime)
• Optional: drizzle 1 tsp Greek‑style lactose‑free yogurt for creaminess.
3 cups air‑popped, tossed with a pinch of smoked paprika and a drizzle of olive oil.

• 150 g lean ground beef
• 1 cup diced potatoes (pre‑roasted)
• ½ cup canned diced tomatoes
• ½ cup kidney beans (rinsed)
• 1 tsp chili powder, ½ tsp cumin, ¼ tsp smoked paprika, pinch of cayenne
• Simmer 20 min; serve with chopped fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime.

column1: ### **Day 2 – Thai‑Inspired**

column4: ½ cup gluten‑free oats cooked in ½ cup coconut milk + ½ cup water; stir in 1 tsp red‑curry paste, a drizzle of honey, top with sliced banana and a sprinkle of toasted pumpkin seeds.

column3: **Carrot sticks + hummus** (store‑bought or homemade with chickpeas, tahini, lemon, garlic).

column4:
• 150 g diced chicken thigh
• 1 cup frozen Asian veg (broccoli, carrots, snap peas)
• 1 tsp ginger‑garlic paste
• 1 Tbsp tamari + 1 tsp fish sauce + ½ tsp lime juice + ½ tsp brown sugar
• Serve over ½ cup cooked rice.

• 150 g pork shoulder, cubed
• 1 cup diced sweet potato (pre‑roasted)
• ½ cup coconut milk
• 1 Tbsp red‑curry paste (check gluten‑free)
• ½ cup sliced bell pepper, ¼ cup bamboo shoots (optional)
• Simmer 15‑20 min; finish with fresh basil and lime. Serve with a small scoop of jasmine rice.

column1: ### **Day 3 – Indian Flavors**

column4: ½ cup chickpea flour, ¼ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp cumin, pinch of chili powder, water to make thin batter; stir in chopped spinach, grated carrot; cook on non‑stick pan with a drizzle of oil. Serve with a spoonful of lactose‑free yogurt and cilantro.

column3: **Cucumber slices + mint‑yogurt dip** (lactose‑free yogurt + fresh mint, lemon juice, pinch of salt).

column4:
• 150 g ground lamb (or diced shoulder)
• 1 cup diced potatoes (pre‑roasted)
• ½ cup canned tomatoes
• 1 tsp garam masala, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp coriander powder, ¼ tsp cumin
• ½ cup water, simmer 20 min; finish with fresh cilantro. Serve with ½ cup basmati rice.

column3: **Roasted chickpeas** (toss canned chickpeas with olive oil, smoked paprika, bake 15 min).

column4:
• 150 g firm tofu, pressed & cubed
• 1 cup mixed veg (zucchini, bell pepper, carrots)
• 1 tsp mustard seeds, ½ tsp cumin, pinch of fenugreek (optional), ½ tsp turmeric
• Cook in 1 Tbsp oil, add ¼ cup coconut milk for a creamy finish. Serve with quinoa or rice.

column1: ### **Day 4 – Mexican‑Thai Fusion**

column4:
• 2 fried eggs
• 1 corn tortilla (warm)
• ½ cup black beans (rinsed)
• Salsa verde (tomatillos, jalapeño, cilantro, lime – blend)
• Avocado slices.

• 150 g thinly sliced beef (sirloin) marinated 10 min in tamari, lime juice, garlic, ginger, a pinch of brown sugar
• Quick‑stir‑fry with shredded carrots, bean sprouts
• Spoon into butter‑lettuce cups; garnish with cilantro and chopped peanuts.

column3: **Rice cakes topped with almond butter & sliced strawberries**

column4:
• 150 g cooked shredded chicken
• 1 cup cooked rice (bottom layer)
• ½ cup canned tomato‑enchilada sauce (check for gluten)
• ½ cup corn kernels
• ¼ cup lactose‑free cheese shreds (optional)
• Layer in a baking dish, bake 15 min at 180 °C. Serve with a side salad (mixed greens, lime‑vinaigrette).

column1: ### **Day 5 – Indian Comfort**

column4: 2 eggs scrambled with turmeric, cumin, chopped tomato, green chili, fresh coriander; cook in 1 tsp oil. Serve with 1 slice gluten‑free toast (optional) or a small baked potato.

column3: **Greek‑style lactose‑free yogurt + a drizzle of honey & a sprinkle of toasted cumin seeds**

column4:
• 1 cup cooked chickpeas
• 2 cups fresh spinach (or frozen)
• ½ cup tomato puree
• 1 tsp garam masala, ½ tsp cumin, ¼ tsp chili powder
• Simmer 10 min; finish with a splash of coconut milk. Serve with ½ cup brown rice.

• 150 g pork shoulder roast (season with coriander, cumin, paprika, a touch of turmeric, salt‑free seasoning)
• Roast with diced potatoes, carrots, and a drizzle of olive oil (about 30‑35 min). Slice and serve with a simple cucumber‑mint salad (cucumber, mint, lime juice).

column1: ### **Day 6 – Thai Street‑Food Vibes**

column4: ½ cup cooked rice + ½ cup coconut milk + ½ cup water; simmer to thick consistency. Stir in minced ginger, a splash of fish sauce, chopped green onion **(green tops only, no white part)**, and a soft‑boiled egg.

column3: **Mango slices (if in season) + a pinch of chili powder**

column4:
• 150 g thin beef strips
• 100 g rice noodles (soaked & drained)
• 1 egg, scrambled into the wok
• 1 tsp tamarind paste + 1 Tbsp tamari + 1 tsp palm sugar (or honey)
• Bean sprouts, shredded carrot, chopped cilantro, lime wedges.

• 150 g chicken thigh pieces
• 1 cup coconut milk + 1 cup low‑sodium chicken broth
• 1 tsp ginger‑garlic paste, 2 kebabs lemongrass (optional), ½ tsp galangal (or extra ginger)
• ½ cup sliced mushrooms, ½ cup cherry tomatoes, ½ cup sliced bell pepper
• Finish with juice of 1 lime, fresh cilantro, and a few sliced chilies. Serve with a side of jasmine rice (½ cup).

column1: ### **Day 7 – Mexican Comfort**

column4:
• Warm 2 corn tortillas
• Fill with scrambled eggs, diced roasted potatoes, salsa roja (tomato, jalapeño, cilantro, lime), and a sprinkle of lactose‑free cheese (optional).

• 150 g ground turkey, cooked with cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder
• ½ cup black beans, ½ cup cooked rice, ¼ cup corn, diced tomato, avocado, cilantro, lime.

column3: **Greek‑style lactose‑free yogurt + a few crushed walnuts**

column4:
• 150 g beef chuck, cut into large chunks
• 1 tsp chipotle in adobo (check gluten), ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp oregano, ½ tsp coriander, ½ cup orange juice, ¼ cup low‑sodium beef broth
• Cook on low 6‑8 h (or 2 h pressure cooker). Shred, serve over a small bed of rice, topped with fresh pico de gallo and sliced radish.

column1: 1,"**7‑Day Cheap & Simple Meal Plan**
*(Gluten‑free, lactose‑free, shellfish‑free, onion‑free – mild‑flavoured for a “super‑tasting” palate)*

column5: Key Ingredients (all gluten‑/lactose‑free)
column6: Quick Prep / Cooking Steps

column5: ½ cup gluten‑free rolled oats, 1 cup almond‑milk, ½ banana, 1 Tbsp chia seeds, pinch cinnamon
column6: 1️⃣ Heat almond‑milk, stir in oats, simmer 3‑4 min. Top with sliced banana, chia, cinnamon.

column5: 150 g skinless chicken thigh, ½ cup brown rice (cooked), ¼ cup black beans (canned, rinsed), ½ cup corn, ¼ avocado, 1 Tbsp salsa (no onion), ½ tsp cumin, lime wedge
column6: 1️⃣ Grill/ pan‑fry chicken with cumin, salt‑light. 2️⃣ Assemble bowl: rice, beans, corn, sliced chicken, avocado, salsa, squeeze lime.

column5: 150 g lean beef strips, 1 medium sweet potato (cubed), 1 cup broccoli florets, 1 Tbsp tamari (gluten‑free soy), 1 tsp ginger, 1 tsp garlic, ½ tsp chili flakes, 1 tsp sesame oil
column6: 1️⃣ Boil sweet‑potato cubes 5 min. 2️⃣ Stir‑fry beef in sesame oil, add ginger & garlic. 3️⃣ Add broccoli, potatoes, tamari, chili; cook 4‑5 min.

column5: 2 eggs, ¼ cup diced ham (no nitrates), ½ cup diced bell pepper, ¼ cup spinach, pinch pepper
column6: 1️⃣ Whisk eggs, fold in veggies & ham. 2️⃣ Pour into greased muffin tin, bake 15 min @180 °C.

column5: ½ cup red lentils, ½ cup basmati rice, 1 carrot, 1 celery stalk, 1 tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp coriander, 1 tsp ginger, 4 cup water, pinch salt‑light
column6: 1️⃣ Simmer lentils, carrot, celery with spices 20 min. 2️⃣ Serve over cooked rice.

column5: 2 chicken thighs (skin‑on), 2 medium potatoes (wedged), 1 Tbsp olive oil, ½ tsp rosemary, ½ tsp thyme, ½ tsp garlic powder (onion‑free), lemon zest
column6: 1️⃣ Toss potatoes in oil + herbs, spread on tray. 2️⃣ Place chicken on top, bake 35‑40 min @200 °C.

column5: ½ cup cooked quinoa, 1 cup coconut‑milk, ½ apple diced, 1 Tbsp almond butter, pinch nutmeg
column6: 1️⃣ Warm coconut‑milk, stir in quinoa 3 min. Top with apple, almond butter, nutmeg.

column5: 150 g chicken breast, ½ cup coconut milk (light), 1 cup mixed frozen veggies (peas, carrots, beans), 1 tsp red‑curry paste (no onion), ½ cup jasmine rice
column6: 1️⃣ Sauté chicken, add curry paste, coconut milk, veg. Simmer 10 min. Serve over rice.

column5: 150 g minced beef, 1 large potato (diced), ¼ cup kidney beans, 1 tsp smoked paprika, ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp oregano, 1 Tbsp tomato‑paste, 1 tsp olive oil
column6: 1️⃣ Brown beef, add spices & tomato‑paste. 2️⃣ Add potatoes & beans, cover, simmer 12‑15 min until potatoes tender.

column5: 1 cup frozen berries, ½ banana, 1 cup oat‑milk (gluten‑free), 1 Tbsp hemp seeds, 1 tsp honey (optional)
column6: Blend all, pour into bowl, sprinkle hemp seeds.

column5: 150 g grilled chicken, mixed lettuce, ½ avocado, ½ cucumber, 1 Tbsp olive‑oil vinaigrette (lemon juice, mustard, pepper)
column6: 1️⃣ Toss all ingredients, drizzle vinaigrette.

column5: 150 g pork shoulder (cubed), 1 sweet potato (cubed), 1 carrot, 1 tsp garam masala, ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp cumin, 1 cup canned diced tomatoes (no onion), 2 cup water
column6: 1️⃣ Brown pork, add spices, veg, tomatoes, water. Simmer 30 min.

column5: 2 slices gluten‑free bread, 2 Tbsp peanut butter, ½ banana sliced, pinch cinnamon
column6: Toast, spread, top with banana & cinnamon.

column5: 150 g ground beef, 1 Tbsp tamari, 1 tsp fish sauce, 1 tsp ginger, 1 tsp garlic, ½ cup shredded carrots, lettuce leaves, lime wedges
column6: 1️⃣ Cook beef with ginger & garlic, add tamari & fish sauce. 2️⃣ Spoon into lettuce leaves, garnish with carrots & lime.

column5: 2 large russet potatoes, 150 g lean ground turkey, ¼ cup black beans, ¼ cup corn, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp smoked paprika, 2 Tbsp guacamole (avocado + lime), cilantro (optional)
column6: 1️⃣ Bake potatoes 45 min @200 °C. 2️⃣ Brown turkey with spices, mix beans & corn. 3️⃣ Split potatoes, top with turkey mix, guac, cilantro.

column5: ½ cup chickpea flour, ½ cup water, ¼ cup diced bell pepper, ¼ cup spinach, pinch pepper, 1 tsp olive oil
column6: 1️⃣ Whisk flour & water → batter. Stir in veg. 2️⃣ Cook thin pancakes in oil 2‑3 min/side.

column5: ½ cup jasmine rice, 1 cup coconut‑milk (light), zest & juice of ½ lime, 150 g chicken breast, 1 tsp coriander, 1 tsp olive oil
column6: 1️⃣ Cook rice in coconut‑milk + lime zest. 2️⃣ Grill chicken seasoned with coriander & oil. Serve together.

column5: 150 g beef cubes, 1 cup mixed veg (cauliflower, peas, carrots), ½ cup coconut‑milk, 1 tsp curry powder (onion‑free), ½ tsp turmeric, ½ tsp ginger, ½ cup basmati rice
column6: 1️⃣ Brown beef, add spices, veg, coconut‑milk. Simmer 20 min. Serve over rice.

column5: ½ cup gluten‑free oats, ½ cup lactose‑free yoghurt (coconut or almond), ½ banana mashed, 1 Tbsp walnuts, pinch vanilla
column6: Mix night before, refrigerate. Eat cold.

column5: 150 g chicken breast, ½ cup cooked rice noodles (gluten‑free), ½ cup shredded cabbage, ¼ cup carrots, 1 Tbsp peanut‑butter, 1 tsp tamari, ½ tsp lime juice, ½ tsp ginger
column6: 1️⃣ Grill chicken, slice. 2️⃣ Toss noodles & veg with sauce (peanut‑butter, tamari, lime, ginger). Top with chicken.

column5: 150 g lean ground beef, ½ cup red kidney beans, ½ cup diced tomatoes (no onion), ½ cup corn, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp chili powder, ½ tsp smoked paprika, ½ cup cooked quinoa
column6: 1️⃣ Brown beef, add spices, beans, tomatoes, corn. Simmer 15 min. Serve over quinoa.

column1: ## Nutritional Snapshot
- **Average daily calories:** ~ 1 800 kcal (moderate deficit for gradual weight loss; adjust +/‑ 200 kcal based on activity level).
- **Protein:** 120‑150 g /day (≈ 30 % of calories) – supports muscle maintenance.
- **Carbs:** 180‑210 g /day (≈ 40 % of calories) – mainly complex (rice, potatoes, quinoa, oats).
- **Fat:** 55‑70 g /day (≈ 30 % of calories) – from olive oil, nuts, avocado, coconut milk.
## Grocery List (≈ 7‑day cost for 1 adult – **budget‑friendly**)

Mixed frozen veg (peas, carrots, beans) 2 bags, broccoli 2 heads, bell peppers 3 pcs, carrots 6 pcs, celery 2 stalks, cucumber 2 pcs, lettuce (butter/iceberg) 1 head, spinach 1 bag, cabbage ½ head, cilantro & parsley 1 bunch each, lime 4 pcs, lemon 2 pcs, ginger 1 piece, garlic 1 bulb
Bananas 7‑10 pcs, apples 4 pcs, frozen berries ½ kg

Chia seeds 100 g, hemp seeds 100 g, walnuts 200 g, almond flakes optional, gluten‑free oat‑milk yoghurt 2 pots (for overnight oats)

column1: *Tip:* Buy in bulk where possible (rice, potatoes, lentils, beans) and use frozen veg to keep cost low and waste minimal.
## Practical Tips for Your “Super‑Tasting” Gene & Flavor Preferences
1. **Mild‑but‑bright flavors** – Use fresh herbs (cilantro, parsley, rosemary) and citrus zest/juice to add depth without overwhelming salt or sweetness.
2. **Control salt** – Choose low‑sodium tamari, soy‑free fish sauce, and season with spices + a squeeze of lime instead of extra salt.
3. **Avoid “very sweet”** – Keep honey or sweet sauces to ≤ ½ tsp per serving; let fruit provide natural sweetness.
4. **Texture variety** – Combine crunchy (nuts, roasted potatoes) with soft (mashed sweet‑potato, creamy coconut‑milk) to keep meals interesting.
5. **Batch‑cook staples** – Cook a big pot of rice, quinoa, and boiled potatoes on Sunday; store in the fridge for quick assembly.
6. **One‑pan meals** – Most dinner recipes are 1‑pan or skillet dishes → less cleanup, less time.
## Sample Daily Schedule (optional)

column3: Light snack (handful nuts or fruit) – optional
Lunch (≈ 550 kcal) – assemble from pre‑cooked rice/potatoes

column1: ### Final Word

This plan hits your dietary restrictions, leans on the cuisines you love (Mexican, Thai, Indian), stays low‑salt & low‑sweet, and uses inexpensive, widely‑available ingredients. Feel free to swap a protein (e.g., replace beef with extra chicken) or a veg based on what’s on sale. Adjust portion sizes up or down by ~ 10 % to match your exact calorie target. Enjoy the variety and the simplicity!",GPT-OSS-120B,openai/gpt-oss-120b,GPT-OSS-120B

The plan leans on the cuisines you love – Mexican, Thai, Indian – while keeping salt and sugar low and using bold, balanced flavours that work well for a “super‑taster.”  Each day is ~2 000 kcal, 150 g protein, 200 g carbs, 70 g fat – feel free to add a small snack (e.g., a handful of nuts, a piece of fruit, or a boiled egg) if you need extra calories.)*

## How This Plan Meets Your Needs

column3: All grains are rice, quinoa, gluten‑free oats, corn tortillas, rice noodles; no wheat‑based sauces.
Uses lactose‑free yogurts, coconut milk, almond milk, dairy‑free cheese alternatives.
No shrimp, fish sauce is used only in tiny amounts (you can replace with a pinch of salt + extra lime if you’re uncomfortable).
Recipes rely on garlic, ginger, scallion greens, chives, herbs, and spices for depth.
Salt is kept to ≤½ tsp per dish; sweeteners are optional and limited to ≤1 tsp honey or sugar.
Avoids bitter greens (e.g., kale, arugula) and heavy bitter spices; balances flavors with citrus, fresh herbs, and a touch of natural sweetness (fruit, honey).
Core proteins (ground beef/turkey, chicken thighs, eggs, canned beans) are cheap; starches are bulk rice/potatoes; herbs/spices bought in small jars last months.
Most meals are one‑pot or 2‑step, ≤30 min total cooking time.  Batch‑cook rice/quinoa once for 2‑3 days to save time.
Mexican (tacos, bowls, fajitas), Thai (coconut curry, basil pork, peanut noodles), Indian (dal, tikka, chickpea curry) are each represented twice.
